hypothesis: First-person CTA ('Get My Free Extension') will outperform current third-person CTA, because first-person possessive language creates psychological ownership before the action is taken.

rationale.cta_primary: Multiple A/B test case studies (Unbounce, HubSpot) show first-person CTA copy ('My' vs 'Your') lifts clicks by 10-20%. 'Get' is more action-oriented than 'Add'. Removing 'to Chrome' reduces cognitive load.
